behavior_summary: Handles a fast-area trigger for the Malchir summoning/test event: when invoked it may spawn a daemon NPC (or similar summoned entity), place it near the trigger, run staged animations/effects, produce barks (short dialogue), and update global test state.

Computes spawn placement by reading the triggering item's coordinates and direction, applies direction-based offsets, validates placement with canExistAt, and falls back to alternate offsets if necessary.
Configures the spawned NPC's stats (dexterity, HP) via a utility lookup, creates the NPC/item entity, pops it into the world, and runs visual/spell effects (FREE::29D8, FIRESPEL::*).
Implements Malchir test logic: reads the MalchirTest global to select a success/progression path or a failure/hostile path; increments MalchirTest on success and sets malchirMad on failure.
Manages avatar stasis around critical moments: checks getAvatarInStasis(), sets/unsets stasis as needed to freeze player during the sequence, and clears NPC in-combat flags during cleanup.
Schedules short delays and spawned helper tasks (inline suspended loops and small spawned follow-ups) to run repeated METHOD::15E7 animations and further NPC behavior.
notable_control_flow: Initial branching by incoming event byte and an indirect parameter (used to distinguish enterFastArea / leaveFastArea or sub-events) determines whether to spawn effects or only run animations.
Placement selection: use trigger coordinates + direction-derived offsets -> call canExistAt -> if false, try alternative offsets; if still invalid, set placement-failed flag and skip spawn.

After a successful placement, create NPC and item, set stats (Npc::setDex, Npc::setHp using FREE::1A96), pop item (Item::pop), then spawn visual effects and a suspended animation sequence (METHOD::15E7 loops).

Malchir test branching: read MalchirTest global, if within progression/success range increment it and follow the 'friendly/progression' path (barks, cleanup), otherwise set malchirMad and make daemon hostile (Npc::setTarget) and bark failure lines.
Multiple spawned/inline helper sequences run asynchronously (e.g., U8P_DAEMSPEL::DAEMSPEL::08BF and ::0AD8 and two inline suspended blocks) to play animations and to clear flags after timed delays.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the incoming event byte values (which numeric values correspond to enterFastArea, leaveFastArea, or sub-actions) — inferred but not explicit.
Precise numeric meanings or tables behind FREE::1A96 (stat lookup/randomization) and FREE::29D8 (visual effect parameters) — their effects are inferred from usage.
Exact strings and dialog text used by Item::bark in each branch (barks are observed but literal texts were not fully enumerated in the extraction).
Whether malchirMad distinguishes spawn origin (player vs NPC) in other systems beyond this script — name usage is inferred from context.
Full list of fallback offsets tried and the exact tile/collision rules influenced by canExistAt (tile map constraints were inferred but not enumerated).

main_function.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::0080
main_function.purpose: Respond to a fast-area trigger to potentially summon and position a daemon NPC, play associated animations/effects, enforce avatar stasis during the sequence, run the MalchirTest success/failure logic (advance test or mark daemon hostile), and perform cleanup (dialogue, clear combat flags, destroy temporaries).
entry_conditions: Invoked as a fast-area event handler (enter/leave/sub-event indicated by an incoming event byte).
Receives caller/process id and a pointer to a trigger item/NPC in BP+06h plus an indirect parameter identifying the triggering item or slot; behavior depends on these inputs.
Only spawns the daemon when specific event/sub-event values and/or indirect parameters match expected values (e.g., an indirect param equal to 1 or certain event bytes).

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::0080
function_map.role: Primary fast-area event handler that runs the Malchir daemon summon/test sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::08BF
function_map.role: Spawned/inline subroutine invoked to run a repeated sequence of METHOD::15E7 calls (NPC animations/actions) on the spawned daemon or target NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::0AD8
function_map.role: Small spawned follow-up task executed after a bark to perform delayed behavior or further adjustments to the spawned NPC.

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::04A7_inline
function_map.role: Inline suspended animation/effect sequence that loops METHOD::15E7 calls and performs timed cleanup (clearing flags).

function_map.name: U8P_DAEMSPEL::0849_inline
function_map.role: Second inline suspended animation/effect loop used in another branch of the main routine for timed actions and cleanup.

internal_calls.purpose: Run a spawned animation/action loop on the target NPC after spawn or as part of the sequence.
Create a short delayed follow-up task executed after a bark to continue NPC behavior.
Inline suspended sequence to run repeated METHOD::15E7 actions and then clear in-combat/stasis flags.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the process/actor that triggered the event to decide whether to spawn effects or treat caller specially.
Check whether the player's avatar is currently frozen to avoid double-stasis or to decide to set stasis.
Freeze/unfreeze the avatar around the summon/effect sequence.
Read trigger item coordinates used as a base for spawn placement and canExistAt checks.
Compute direction from trigger to target to select facing and placement offsets for the spawned daemon.
Validate candidate spawn coordinates; used to accept or reject placement and to trigger fallback offsets.
Create the NPC object for the daemon when placement succeeds.
Set the spawned NPC's stats, typically drawing values from FREE::1A96.
Create the in-world item/entity for the daemon and place it at the computed coordinates with facing/flags.
Lookup or derive numeric stat values used when configuring the spawned NPC.
Spawn visual/particle/spell effects associated with the summon or branch-specific visuals.
Invoke NPC animation/action routines repeatedly (spawned) on the target or spawned daemon as part of the sequence.
Set a per-item mode/state byte on the spawned item/NPC (configure behavior/state).
Update interactions, clean up temporary items, and branch logic depending on whether a reference is an NPC.
On failure/hostile branch set daemon to attack a target and clear/reset combat flags during cleanup.
Produce dialogue/bark strings to narrate success, progression, or failure to the player.
Schedule short delays between sequences (timing helper).

reads.name: MalchirTest (global [0165 03])
reads.likely_effect: Selects which Malchir test branch runs (success/progression or failure) based on its numeric value.

reads.name: malchirMad (global [0162 02])
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine prior state about daemon hostility/spawn origin; affects branching for later behavior.

writes.name: MalchirTest (global [0165 03])
writes.likely_effect: Incremented/modified during a successful test to advance the MalchirTest progress stage.

writes.name: malchirMad (global [0162 02])
writes.likely_effect: Set when the test fails to mark the spawned daemon as 'mad' or hostile (used to trigger attack behavior).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles a fast-area trigger for the Malchir summoning/test event: when invoked it may spawn a daemon NPC (or similar summoned entity), place it near the trigger, run staged animations/effects, produce barks (short dialogue), and update global test state.
